title: Australia's Grid Transition: What 25 Years of NEM Data Reveals
summary: A long-run view of Australia's electricity transition, connecting generation, emissions intensity, market value and seasonal system behaviour.
key_takeaway: Australia's transition is visible in the data: coal is declining while wind and solar become material contributors, making storage, transmission and flexible demand central to the next phase.

inline_figures.caption: Monthly generation patterns vary by technology, making storage, transmission and flexible demand essential system tools.
Carbon intensity differs sharply by generation technology, reinforcing why the composition of supply matters as much as total output.
